The sacred anointing that was used then pointed to the great Messiah, or Anointed One, the King of the church, and High Priest of our profession, who was anointed with the Spirit’s oil, not by measure but without limit, and above all the priests and princes of the Jewish church. For Saul's further satisfaction, Samuel gave him some signs that would come to pass on that same day. The first place he directed him to was the sepulchre of one of his ancestors; there he must be reminded of his own mortality, and now that he had a crown before him, must think of his grave, in which all his honor would be laid in the dust. From the time of Samuel there appears to have been schools, or places where pious young men were brought up in the knowledge of Divine things. Saul should find himself strongly moved to join with them, and should be transformed from what he had been. The Spirit of God changes people, wonderfully transforms them. Saul, by praising God in the company of other believers, became a different person, but it may be questioned if he became a new person.

The signs Samuel had given Saul came to pass exactly as predicted; he found that God had given him another heart, another disposition of mind. Yet let not an outward show of devotion and a sudden change for the present be too much relied on; Saul among the prophets was still Saul. His being anointed was kept private. He left it to God to carry on his own work through Samuel and sat still to see how the matter would unfold.

Samuel tells the people, You have rejected your God today. Saul now seemed so little fond of that power which soon after, when he possessed it, he could not think of giving up, that he hid himself. It is good to be conscious of our unworthiness and insufficiency for the services to which we are called; but people should not go into the opposite extreme by refusing the employments to which the Lord and the church call them. The greater part of the people treated the matter with indifference. Saul modestly went home to his own house, but was attended by a group of men whose hearts God disposed to support his authority. If the heart bends at any time in the right way, it is because He has touched it. One touch is enough when it is from God. Others despised him. Thus differently are people affected toward our exalted Redeemer. There is a remnant who submit to him and follow him wherever he goes; they are those whose hearts God has touched, whom he has made willing. But there are others who despise him, who ask, How shall this man save us? They are offended in him, and they will be punished.
